description_long: This is a two-story half-timbered house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in textured, weathered shingles. The exterior walls feature a classic timber frame pattern with white infill panels, giving it a traditional European or Tudor appearance. The front facade has a central wooden door with a small stone step and two vertically aligned windows on each floor, all with gridded panes. The side elevations display symmetrical rows of windows, and the rear mirrors the front but lacks a door. Two prominent stone chimneys rise from the roof ridge, adding to the historic character. The house sits on a rectangular stone or cobblestone base, suggesting an old-world urban or village setting.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and two stone chimneys, evoking a traditional European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two rows of gridded windows, and the stone step leading to the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, symmetrical window arrangement, and both stone chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view with two rows of windows similar to the front but without a door, and the roof's ridge visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, showing the gable roof, symmetrical windows, and both chimneys.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with a small stone step, no porch or overhang, flanked by gridded windows.
